examples: She is wearing a blue dress.

standard.notes: Always singular in French (le pantalon), unlike English 'trousers/pants'.

examples: He is wearing a big hat.

standard.notes: Same form for masculine and feminine: un sac rouge, une robe rouge.
Feminine form: blanche. Blanc → blanche is an irregular change.

examples: She is wearing a blue dress.

advanced.notes: Always singular in French (le pantalon), unlike English 'trousers/pants'.

examples: He is wearing a big hat.

advanced.notes: Same form for masculine and feminine: un sac rouge, une robe rouge.
Feminine form: blanche. Blanc → blanche is an irregular change.
Usually plural. Singular: la chaussure (one shoe).
Plural: les manteaux (add -x). Worn in cold weather.
examples: Il fait froid, prends ton manteau.

dialogue: Qu'est-ce que tu portes pour la fête ?
Je ne sais pas. La robe rouge ou le pantalon noir ?
La robe rouge est très jolie !
Oui, mais il fait froid ce soir.
Tu peux porter la robe avec un manteau.
Bonne idée ! Et toi, tu portes quoi ?
Un pantalon blanc et un chapeau.

questions.question: Qu'est-ce que B hésite à porter ?
options: Une robe rouge ou un pantalon noir
questions.correctAnswer: Une robe rouge ou un pantalon noir

questions.question: Que porte A ?
options: Un pantalon blanc et un chapeau
questions.correctAnswer: Un pantalon blanc et un chapeau

standard.summary: Two friends discuss what to wear to a party. B hesitates between a red dress and black trousers because it's cold. A suggests wearing the dress with a coat. A will wear white trousers and a hat.

dialogue: Tu es prête pour la fête ?
Presque. Je ne sais pas quelles chaussures porter.
Avec ta robe bleue ? Les chaussures noires sont bien.
Oui, mais elles ne sont pas très confortables. Et les blanches ?
Les blanches avec une robe bleue ? C'est joli. Mets ton manteau gris aussi.
Le gris ? Non, je préfère le manteau noir. Il est plus élégant.
D'accord. Moi, je porte un pantalon vert et une chemise blanche.
Vert et blanc ? C'est un bon choix. On y va !

questions.question: Quel est le problème de B ?
options: Elle ne sait pas quelles chaussures porter.
questions.correctAnswer: Elle ne sait pas quelles chaussures porter.

advanced.summary: Two friends get ready for a party. B has a blue dress and debates between black shoes (uncomfortable) and white ones. She picks the black coat over the grey one. A wears a green trouser and white shirt combination.

standard.title: Adjective Agreement & Placement (masculine/feminine, before/after noun)
standard.explanation: In French, adjectives must agree in gender and number with the noun they describe. Most adjectives add -e for feminine and -s for plural: petit → petite → petits → petites. Colour adjectives go AFTER the noun: une robe rouge (a red dress), un chapeau blanc (a white hat). Some common short adjectives go BEFORE the noun (BANGS rule): beau, autre, nouveau, grand, petit. Example: un grand chapeau, une petite robe. Irregular feminines: blanc → blanche, beau → belle, nouveau → nouvelle.

drills.question: Un chapeau ___. (red)
drills.explanation: Rouge is the same for masculine and feminine.

drills.question: Where does the colour go in French?
drills.explanation: Colour adjectives go after the noun: une robe rouge, not une rouge robe.

drills.question: Un ___ chapeau. (big)
drills.explanation: Grand goes before the noun (BANGS adjective).

advanced.title: Adjective Agreement — Regular, Irregular & BANGS Rule
advanced.explanation: Regular agreement: add -e (feminine), -s (plural), -es (feminine plural): vert → verte → verts → vertes. Irregular feminines: blanc/blanche, gris/grise, long/longue, beau/belle, nouveau/nouvelle, vieux/vieille. BANGS adjectives go BEFORE the noun: Beauty (beau, joli), Age (jeune, vieux, nouveau), Number (premier, dernier), Goodness (bon, mauvais, gentil), Size (grand, petit, gros, long). Multiple adjectives: size before noun + colour after: un grand manteau noir. Colour adjectives ending in -e don't change: rouge, jaune, rose.

drills.question: Which is correct?
drills.explanation: Colour after noun, agree in gender: une robe rouge.

drills.question: Une ___ robe. (beautiful)
drills.explanation: Beau → belle (feminine). BANGS: goes before noun.

drills.question: Which adjective does NOT change in the feminine?
drills.explanation: Rouge already ends in -e, so it stays the same for masculine and feminine.

drills.question: Le ___ manteau gris. (old)
drills.explanation: Vieux = old (masculine). Feminine: vieille. Goes before the noun.

standard.question: What does 'la robe' mean?
standard.explanation: 'La robe' means the dress. It is feminine.

standard.question: Le chapeau est ___. (white, masculine)
standard.explanation: Blanc is the masculine form. Feminine: blanche.

standard.question: How do you say 'a red dress'?
standard.explanation: Colour goes after the noun. Robe is feminine: une robe rouge.

standard.question: What is 'le pantalon'?
standard.explanation: 'Le pantalon' means trousers/pants. Singular in French.

standard.question: Il porte un grand ___. (hat)
standard.explanation: 'Le chapeau' means the hat. Plural: les chapeaux.

advanced.question: Il fait froid, prends ton ___. (coat)
advanced.explanation: 'Le manteau' means the coat. Plural: les manteaux.

advanced.question: What is the feminine of 'blanc'?
advanced.explanation: Blanc → blanche is an irregular feminine change.

advanced.question: Where does 'grand' go in a sentence?
advanced.explanation: Grand is a BANGS adjective — it goes before the noun: un grand manteau.

advanced.question: Une ___ robe bleue. (beautiful)
advanced.explanation: Beau → belle (feminine). BANGS adjective: before the noun.

standard.scenario: Your friend asks what you're wearing today. Describe your outfit — mention at least three clothing items and their colours.
standard.modelAnswer: Aujourd'hui, je porte un pantalon noir et une chemise blanche. Mon chapeau est rouge. Mes chaussures sont noires.
standard.modelAnswerExplanation: This uses 'je porte' for wearing, colour adjectives after the noun, and agreement: blanc → blanche (feminine), noir → noires (feminine plural).
standard.tip: Remember: colours go after the noun. Use 'blanc/blanche' and 'noir/noire' depending on gender.

advanced.scenario: You're helping a friend choose an outfit for an important dinner. Describe what they should wear, suggest colours that go well together, and explain why you recommend each item. Use adjectives before and after the nouns.
advanced.modelAnswer: Pour le dîner, tu peux porter ta belle robe bleue. Elle est élégante. Avec la robe, les chaussures noires vont bien. Prends ton nouveau manteau gris, il fait froid ce soir. Le blanc va aussi bien avec le bleu, mais le noir est plus élégant.
advanced.modelAnswerExplanation: This uses BANGS adjectives before nouns (belle robe, nouveau manteau), colours after nouns (robe bleue, chaussures noires), comparative (plus élégant), and 'aller bien avec' for matching.
